summary: eSports Tracker: an Internet Computer (DFINITY) dApp for the eSports ecosystem with two distinct user modes: Players and Teams. Players create/edit profiles, post daily structured progress updates with optional clips, and build readiness/fit signals for hiring. Teams create/manage org profiles, post hiring requirements (with readiness/Fit gates), and use a Talent Finder to search/filter players. The app includes structured endorsements (credibility signals) and performance/deployment diagnostics UI. Authentication direction is shifting from Internet Identity to MVP-only Google OAuth identity with a one-time immutable role selection enforced by backend authorization.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication (login/logout + persisted session)
existing_features.description: Provides authentication via Internet Identity using @dfinity/auth-client. Restores stored sessions on reload, exposes login() and clear() (logout), and tracks login lifecycle status for UI.

existing_features.title: Identity-scoped backend actor initialization with RBAC bootstrap secret
existing_features.description: Creates an anonymous actor when unauthenticated and an identity-bound actor when authenticated. On authenticated creation, calls _initializeAccessControlWithSecret using a secret token read from the URL hash (caffeineAdminToken) to bootstrap roles.

existing_features.title: Role-based access control (admin/user/guest) with admin token environment bootstrap
existing_features.description: Motoko access-control assigns roles and enforces permissions via hasPermission checks. First caller providing the correct secret (matching CAFFEINE_ADMIN_TOKEN) becomes admin; others become user. Includes admin role checks and role assignment endpoint.

existing_features.title: Authenticated routing with profile gating (setup vs dashboards)
existing_features.description: After login, fetches the caller profile; routes to role/profile setup when missing, otherwise routes to PlayerDashboard or TeamDashboard based on userType. Uses React.lazy + Suspense for code splitting and loading fallbacks.

existing_features.title: Role selection onboarding (Player vs Team)
existing_features.description: New users choose Player or Team mode before filling the corresponding setup form. Setup is skipped if a profile already exists.

existing_features.title: Player profile creation and editing
existing_features.description: Players can create and update their profile (username, primary game, role/position, skill level, open-to-team, readiness requirement). Backend enforces username non-empty, readiness range 0–100, and prevents changing userType after creation.

existing_features.title: Team profile creation and management
existing_features.description: Teams can create/update a recruiting profile (team name, games recruiting, description, requirements, contact info). Backend ensures team-only behavior and auto-creates a corresponding #team user profile record if missing.

existing_features.title: Readiness metrics and readiness requirement management
existing_features.description: Backend exposes readiness metrics and supports updating a player’s readiness requirement (0–100). Frontend displays readiness banners with progress bars and a modal explaining the score (currently static explanation).

existing_features.title: Progress posts (create post, feed, and user timeline)
existing_features.description: Players can create progress posts with improvement text; the app provides a global feed and per-user timelines. Posts are timestamped and displayed with author info and relative date formatting.

existing_features.title: Gameplay clip upload and playback for posts
existing_features.description: Post creation supports optional gameplay video clips (validated as video/* and max 50MB). The frontend uploads using ExternalBlob.fromBytes() with progress callbacks; PostCard renders clips in an HTML5 video player via a direct URL.

existing_features.title: Team hiring requirement posting and listing
existing_features.description: Teams can post hiring requirements specifying game, role, skill level, minimum readiness score, and requirement text. Teams can list their own hiring requirements; backend restricts creation to team users.

existing_features.title: Player-side team search (search all hiring requirements)
existing_features.description: Players can search for teams/hiring requirements using a prominent search bar plus filters (game, role, skill level, minimum readiness score). Includes clear-search and clear-all behaviors and loading/empty/result states.

existing_features.title: Team-side talent finder (player discovery with filters and text search)
existing_features.description: Teams can search players using filters (game, role, level, minimum readiness score, open-to-team only, has-clip only) plus a text search query. UI includes clear actions, filter card, and consistent results header/empty/loading states.

existing_features.title: Endorsement system (submit, delete, list, and summary)
existing_features.description: Users can endorse players with predefined endorsement types or a custom text endorsement. Backend prevents self-endorsement and enforces one endorsement per endorser per player; supports deletion by the endorser. Frontend provides an endorsement dialog and renders aggregated endorsement counts on profiles and in talent cards.

existing_features.title: Role-based dashboards and navigation (Player vs Team)
existing_features.description: Player dashboard supports Progress feed, Find Teams, and Profile views, with conditional feed fetching and a floating action button to create posts. Team dashboard supports Home, Profile, Hiring, and Talent sections, plus logout menus that clear identity and React Query cache.

existing_features.title: Blob-storage operations mixin (gateway authorization, cashier refill, dead blob pruning)
existing_features.description: Backend includes maintenance endpoints to update authorized gateway principals, refill the storage cashier canister with cycles, list dead blobs for deletion, confirm deletion (and trigger GC), and return a simple upload certificate stub.

existing_features.title: Deployment status UI via build-time metadata
existing_features.description: Reads build-time environment variables to display deployment success/failure/unknown state, timestamps, canister/frontend URLs, error messages, and log links on the login/loading surfaces without requiring backend changes.

existing_features.title: Performance diagnostics markers and debug overlay
existing_features.description: Records app initialization/auth milestones using performance.now() and optionally displays elapsed times in a small overlay when the URL contains ?perf=1.

existing_features.title: Implement an MVP-only Google-style identity login flow that results in a backend session for the caller and stores only identity fields (email, name, avatar) without assigning any role during login.

Update the frontend login page to use a Google-style login button and, after login, route users based on backend `getCurrentUser()` as follows: if `userType` is null redirect to `/select-role`; if player go to Player onboarding; if team go to Team onboarding.

Create/adjust a dedicated role-selection route `/select-role` that is mandatory when `userType` is null and includes exactly two role choices (Player / Team) and a clear warning that the choice is permanent; selecting a role calls `setUserType` and then routes to the appropriate onboarding flow.

Add frontend routing guards so that authenticated users with `userType` unset (null) are always redirected to `/select-role`, and users with `userType` set are prevented from accessing `/select-role` (redirect to their correct onboarding/dashboard).

new_feature_requests.summary: Implement MVP-only Google OAuth login (identity only) + mandatory one-time immutable role selection (player/team) with backend-enforced role-based API authorization and frontend routing guards.

Add a backend User model (separate from Player/Team profile models) with fields `id`, `email`, `name`, `avatar`, and `userType` where `userType` is nullable (null | player | team), and store it per authenticated caller.
Implement backend APIs `getCurrentUser()` and `setUserType(userType)` where `setUserType` can only be called once per user and fails if `userType` is already set.

Enforce role-based access control in the backend so that player-only APIs reject team users and team-only APIs reject player users, using the stored immutable `userType` as the source of truth (not client-provided parameters).

architectural_notes: Frontend is React + TypeScript (Vite) using @tanstack/react-query for all backend queries/mutations with cache invalidation on mutation success.
Backend is a single Motoko canister actor (backend/main.mo) composed with mixins for authorization (MixinAuthorization) and blob-storage utilities (MixinStorage).
Role-based access control (admin/user/guest) is bootstrapped via a secret token compared against the CAFFEINE_ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable.
Frontend actor creation is identity-scoped via a dedicated useActor hook; authenticated actor initialization calls _initializeAccessControlWithSecret using a URL-hash secret (caffeineAdminToken).
Domain modeling uses Motoko variant types (Game/Role/Level/UserType) mirrored to the frontend via generated candid bindings.
ExternalBlob is used for video clip uploads; frontend constructs ExternalBlob from bytes and tracks upload progress; UI plays clips via direct URL.
Search experiences are server-side filtered (talent search; hiring requirement search) with memoized filter objects and stable serialized query keys to reduce refetch churn (staleTime/gcTime tuned).
UI is built with Tailwind CSS + shadcn-style component primitives and lucide-react icons; dashboards use tab/view state rather than URL routing.
Deployment observability is surfaced purely via build-time environment variables (VITE_DEPLOYMENT_*) rendered in a DeploymentStatusCard.
A production-safe performance marker utility (perfDiagnostics) records app milestones and can be viewed via a ?perf=1 overlay.
known_issues: Backend state is in-memory Maps/vars (no stable persistence shown), so canister upgrades/reinstalls would wipe profiles, posts, hiring requirements, and endorsements.
Blob-storage cashier env var key is spelled CAFFFEINE_STORAGE_CASHIER_PRINCIPAL (extra 'F'); misconfiguration will trap at runtime.
SystemHiringRequirement snapshots teamName/contactInfo at hiring-post creation time and is not updated if the team edits its profile later.
searchAllHiringRequirementsForPlayers returns filtered results without explicit sorting (order may be unstable/unexpected).
useActor invalidates and also refetches nearly all queries on actor change, which can cause refetch storms and slow initial loads on auth transitions.
TalentFinder renders endorsement badges by calling useGetPlayerEndorsementSummary per player card (potential N+1 query/performance issue on large result sets).
TalentFinder 'View Profile' button is present but does not navigate (no routing/action implemented in the component).
ReadinessBreakdownModal shows static placeholder breakdown values rather than a backend-derived computation.
Two different CSS entrypoints exist (frontend/index.css and frontend/src/index.css), which can cause confusion about the active design tokens/theme source.
